opportunities.title: Referrals & Appointments national funding
opportunities.summary: National funding for NHS providers to scale the Booking and Referral Standard (BaRS), the e-Referral Service (e-RS), and the NHS App Wayfinder in 2026/27. Covers PIFU rollout, notifications, GIRFT pathway redesign, and Innovation Lead roles spanning divisions or whole systems.
opportunities.means: Not a tender. A trust-facing pot from NHS England to fund adoption of existing national platforms (BaRS, e-RS, NHS App). Deadline is 8 days away. The trusts that win these are the ones who already have a supplier lined up and a delivery plan signed by a clinical SRO. If you supply into any of these stacks, your trust customers should already know whether they're bidding. Call them this week. If you don't have those relationships, the window is too short for new ones.

opportunities.title: Digital Medicines Management national funding
opportunities.summary: National revenue pot for NHS trusts to extend EPR and ePMA capability across two themes: medicines on admission (consuming structured GP medicines via GP Connect) and closed-loop medicines management (end-to-end interoperability across EPR, ePMA, automated dispensing cabinets and pharmacy stock control).
opportunities.means: Follow-on to the digital medicines first-of-type schemes. Trust-facing on paper, supplier-dependent in practice. Applications expect evidence of supplier commitment, the commercial approach, and partnership with other trusts on the same stack. That last point is the tell: NHS England wants supplier-anchored consortia, not lone trusts. EPMA, GP Connect specialists, and dispensing-automation vendors should be the ones starting these conversations, not the trusts. Applications open early June and close 17 July at 5pm. If you supply EPMA or closed-loop stack, get three or more trust customers aligned in the next two weeks.

opportunities.title: Healthcare AI Solutions Framework
opportunities.summary: National framework for healthcare AI across eight lots: radiology and imaging, pathology and early detection, virtual and robotic health, predictive analytics, research and development, operational efficiency, advisory and specialised support, and combined solutions.
opportunities.means: Updated 23 May: value lifted to £900m incl VAT, up from the original £150m estimate and the £750m figure carried earlier. A six-fold jump after supplier engagement that signals NHS SBS is expecting heavy lot-level competition. The procurement route for UK healthcare AI for the next four years. Lots 6 and 7 (operational efficiency, advisory) remain the quieter opportunity. Enquiry deadline 29 May, tender deadline 23 June. Tight: clinical evidence packs and DCB0129 documentation take weeks.

opportunities.title: Tech Innovation Framework / GP IT Futures
opportunities.summary: Assured core GP IT systems and digital products for primary care, bought via the Digital Care Services catalogue. Tech Innovation Framework is the successor to GP IT Futures.
opportunities.means: Essential route for anything touching primary care. GP IT Futures continuity contracts run only to June 2026. If primary care is a target market, getting onto TIF is the gating step.

opportunities.title: MedTech Funding Mandate
opportunities.summary: Mandates NHS commissioners and providers to fund a defined list of NICE-approved cost-saving devices, diagnostics and digital products.
opportunities.means: Not a tender. It's a route to guaranteed local funding. Eligibility: positive NICE guidance, net saving within three years, budget impact under £20m a year. Worth understanding early if a NICE pathway is in reach.

opportunities.title: G-Cloud (Digital Marketplace)
opportunities.summary: Cross-government framework for cloud hosting, software (SaaS) and digital support. The primary procurement route for software-based digital health products.
opportunities.means: The most relevant framework for digital health SaaS. Being listed makes a product discoverable to every NHS buyer. Application windows open periodically, worth tracking.

opportunities.title: Spark DPS: Innovative Health & Care Solutions
opportunities.summary: DPS for innovative SME-developed health and care products and services. Lower barrier to entry than a full framework, suppliers can join at any point.
opportunities.means: The SME-friendly route into NHS procurement. Lower paperwork burden than a framework, and you can join any time, not just at a renewal window. Best fit for devices and remote monitoring; weaker for pure SaaS (use G-Cloud instead).

opportunities.title: Vertical Application Solutions (RM6259)
opportunities.summary: Cross-government framework for sector-specific software. Lot 1 covers Health, Welfare and Community products, including EPRs, patient management and population health platforms.
opportunities.means: The right framework for full EPR or sector-specific platforms that don't fit G-Cloud's SaaS shape. Call-offs are bigger and slower-moving than G-Cloud, but commitments are longer.

opportunities.title: NOE CPC Digital & IT Frameworks
opportunities.source: NOE CPC (NHS North of England Commercial Procurement Collaborative)
opportunities.summary: Northern NHS procurement hub running a portfolio of digital and IT frameworks. Member trusts across the North of England buy directly from these.
opportunities.means: If your target buyers are northern trusts, NOE CPC frameworks reach them faster than national routes. Part of NHS Procurement in Partnership, so being listed on one hub often surfaces you to all four.

opportunities.title: NHS LPP Digital & Healthtech Frameworks
opportunities.summary: London-led NHS procurement hub running digital, workforce and clinical frameworks. Member trusts across London and the South East access these directly.
opportunities.means: London concentration of trusts means a single LPP framework reach can be significant. Good complement to NOE CPC for full-England coverage.

opportunities.title: NHS Procurement in Partnership (PiP)
opportunities.source: NHS PiP (NOE CPC, NHS LPP, NHS Commercial Solutions, East of England NHS Collaborative)
opportunities.summary: Federation of the four NHS regional procurement collaboratives. Member trusts can access the framework portfolios of all four hubs.
opportunities.means: Worth knowing how this federation works. Being on one hub's framework can mean reach across all four hub regions, depending on call-off terms. Reduces the duplication of selling into multiple regional hubs.

opportunities.title: Network Services 3 (RM6116)
opportunities.summary: Cross-government framework for network, telecoms and connectivity services. Includes NHS-relevant call-offs for HSCN connectivity, secure messaging and unified comms.
opportunities.means: Only relevant if your product needs HSCN-grade networking or telephony integration. Otherwise stick to G-Cloud. Useful for clinical comms platforms riding on top of NHS network stacks.

opportunities.title: Clinical Communications Procurement Framework
opportunities.summary: National framework for clinical communication tools including secure messaging, task management, and care coordination platforms. Successor route to legacy bleep replacement.
opportunities.means: If you are in the secure messaging or clinical task management space, this is the procurement route. Trusts use this to replace bleeps and pagers, a large active market.

opportunities.title: NHS Innovation Accelerator (NIA)
opportunities.summary: National accelerator for proven innovations seeking adoption across the NHS. Fellows get sponsorship, NHS connections, and a credibility marker, not direct procurement.
opportunities.means: Not a tender. It is a credibility and access route for products with at least one NHS deployment. Cohort applications open annually. Worth applying if you have evidence and want NHS visibility at scale.

opportunities.title: DigitalHealth.London Accelerator
opportunities.summary: 12-month accelerator for SMEs with digital products solving NHS challenges in London. Includes NHS navigator support, evidence generation help, and direct trust introductions.
opportunities.means: Most useful if your product is at the post-pilot stage and needs structured introductions to London ICS commissioners. Applications usually open in the spring.

opportunities.title: Innovation Technology Payment (ITP), legacy
opportunities.summary: Legacy. The Innovation Technology Payment ran selected high-impact innovations on a national tariff. The programme has wound down. The MedTech Funding Mandate (MTFM) is its functional successor for cost-saving NICE-approved technologies.
opportunities.means: Listed here for context only. If you were considering ITP as a route, the live equivalent is the MedTech Funding Mandate. See the MTFM entry.

opportunities.title: AI Airlock (MHRA regulatory sandbox)
opportunities.source: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency
opportunities.summary: MHRA sandbox for AI as a Medical Device (AIaMD) developers. Lets you work through regulatory questions with MHRA before market authorisation.
opportunities.means: Not procurement. It is regulatory derisking. If your product is an AIaMD heading for UKCA / CE marking, joining a cohort is the most efficient way to align with the regulator early. Cohort calls open periodically.

opportunities.title: NHS England Digital Care Services Catalogue
opportunities.summary: Catalogue of assured digital products for primary care, including GP IT systems, prescribing and patient management tools. Successor to the GP Systems of Choice framework.
opportunities.means: If primary care is in scope, this is the assurance catalogue you need to be on. Gating step for adoption by GP practices. Application is non-trivial: cybersecurity, clinical safety, data interoperability requirements all need to pass.

opportunities.title: GP Systems of Choice (GPSoC)
opportunities.summary: The framework that ran GP IT systems procurement from 2014 to 2018. Superseded by GP IT Futures, which is itself transitioning to the Tech Innovation Framework.
opportunities.means: Closed, but instructive. Knowing the GPSoC, GP IT Futures, TIF lineage helps founders read where primary care procurement is heading next. The pattern: each generation widens supplier eligibility while raising the assurance bar.

opportunities.title: Health Systems Support Framework (HSSF)
opportunities.summary: NHS England framework for population health management, digital and analytics. NHS England has stopped initiating new call-offs but existing ones continue. A successor framework has been signalled.
opportunities.means: Worth tracking the successor announcement. HSSF supported a lot of population health platform work. Whatever replaces it will set the procurement route for the next generation of population health and PHM tools.

opportunities.title: NHSX AI in Health and Care Award (legacy rounds)
opportunities.summary: Four-round AI award programme that funded NHS deployments of AI technologies across imaging, decision support and operational use cases. Rounds 1-4 ran 2020-2023; programme now wound into the NIHR pathway.
opportunities.means: Closed, but the awarded companies are a who's-who of UK AI healthtech. The award structure (development through to real-world evaluation) became the template for NHS-aligned AI funding. Worth knowing the alumni for partnership and patterns.
